content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
December 31, 1992

Robert E. Leidigh
Olson, Connelly, Hagel, Fong &
Leidigh
Capitol Bank Center
300 Capitol Mall, Suite 350
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Your Request for Confirmation
of Telephone Advice
Our File No. I-92-635

Dear Mr. Leidigh:

This is in response to your request for confirmation of
telephone advice concerning the lobbying disclosure provisions of
the Political Reform Act.¹ Because you have not identified the
client on whose behalf you are writing, we are treating your
request as one for informal assistance.²

In our conversation you indicated that a trade association,
in addition to various duties performed for its members, raised
revenue through an annual convention. For income tax purposes,
the trade association created a separate "for profit", wholly
owned subsidiary corporate entity to expand its income-generating
activities without endangering the tax exempt status of the trade
association.

The wholly owned subsidiary, in addition to its income-
generating activities, is also contracting with the trade
association to provide other staff services including the
employment of two in-house lobbyists and the services of an
outside lobbying firm.

_____________

¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

² Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sections
18329(c)(3).)

428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento, CA 95812 ● (916) 322-5660

Leidigh, Robert E.
I-92-635
Page 2


I advised you to register the trade association and the
wholly owned subsidiary as one lobbyist employer, reflecting both
names on the registration. The in-house lobbyists will continue
to file as such, showing the two entities as their sole employer.
The outside lobbying firm will show the two entities as a single
employer.

If you have additional questions, please contact me at (916)
322-5662.

Sincerely,

[signature]
By: Wayne P. Imberi
Political Reform Consultant

sections.question_synthetic: How should a trade association and its wholly owned for-profit subsidiary register as a lobbying employer when the subsidiary employs in-house lobbyists and contracts with an outside lobbying firm?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The trade association and its wholly owned subsidiary should register together as a single lobbyist employer under both names. In-house lobbyists should list both entities as their sole employer, and the outside lobbying firm should also show both entities as a single employer.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Informal advice letter confirming prior telephone guidance; no formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present; document lacks traditional section headers; facts and advice are embedded in body text

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: How should a trade association and its wholly owned for-profit subsidiary register as a lobbying employer when the subsidiary employs in-house lobbyists and contracts with an outside lobbying firm?

CONCLUSION: The trade association and its wholly owned subsidiary should register together as a single lobbyist employer under both names. In-house lobbyists should list both entities as their sole employer, and the outside lobbying firm should also show both entities as a single employer.

embedding.summary: FPPC confirms telephone advice regarding lobbying disclosure registration for a trade association and its wholly owned for-profit subsidiary that employs lobbyists and contracts for lobbying services.
